prompt: Run a design critique on our ManageCPMRules page. Read the file at src/components/ManageCPMRulesPage.jsx — this is where ad ops managers at retailers set CPM floor prices and yield optimization rules. It's one of the most business-critical screens in the platform. Tell me honestly if it's good enough or if it needs major work.

assertions.name: reads_actual_source_file
assertions.description: Critique references specific elements from ManageCPMRulesPage.jsx (not generic placeholders)
assertions.check: file contains specific component names, props, or JSX patterns from the actual source file

assertions.name: produces_rigor_matrix_table
assertions.description: Output contains the full Rigor Matrix with all four dimensions
assertions.check: file contains '| Feasibility' AND '| UX' AND '| Business' AND '| Delight'

assertions.name: has_verdict_section
assertions.description: Output includes an actionable Verdict section

assertions.name: ux_cites_specific_friction
assertions.description: UX analysis names specific friction points (not generic 'needs improvement')
assertions.check: qualitative — reviewer checks that UX section names specific UI patterns or interactions

assertions.name: business_connects_to_yield
assertions.description: Business analysis connects CPM rules to yield optimization or revenue impact
assertions.check: file contains 'yield' or 'revenue' or 'CPM' in business analysis section

assertions.name: covers_all_four_lenses
assertions.description: All four agent sections are present in the output
assertions.check: file contains 'Feasibility Analysis' AND 'UX Analysis' AND 'Business Analysis' AND 'Delight Analysis'
